hook.text: Not all tropical forests are rainforests. Some survive months without a single drop of rain.

buildup.text: Tropical dry forests shed their leaves in dry season to conserve water—bare canopies for months.

discovery.text: When rains return, the forest explodes back to life in weeks—green, flowering, and humming.

twist.text: Dry forests are easier to clear than rainforests, so they've been hit harder by agriculture.

climax.text: Over 60% of tropical dry forests worldwide have been converted or severely degraded.

punchline.text: The forgotten tropical forest is the most endangered.

quiz.question: How do tropical dry forests survive drought?
options: Trees drop their leaves to conserve water
They grow deeper into the ocean
They shift to photosynthesis at night
